insight: Bosnia-Herzegovina's performance after hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup shows a mixed trend, with the team gaining momentum in only half of the breaks, resulting in a slight decline in performance on average. When they were pressing before a break, they tended to lose momentum afterwards, with a significant decline in performance, which happened in the only instance where they were pressing. However, when they weren't pressing before a break, they were able to maintain their performance level, as seen in the one instance where their momentum remained steady. This suggests that Bosnia-Herzegovina may need to adjust their strategy when pressing before a hydration break to avoid losing momentum and instead use the break to regroup and maintain their performance.
